Meteoalarm.WeatherWarning.name: WeatherWarning
Meteoalarm.WeatherWarning.description: A severe weather warning from the EUMETNET Meteoalarm system, aggregating warnings from 30+ European national meteorological services. Each warning follows the CAP (Common Alerting Protocol) structure with awareness levels and hazard types.

Meteoalarm.Warnings.mqtt.description: MQTT/5.0 transport variant for Meteoalarm CAP weather warnings. Non-retained QoS-1 warning events route by country feed slug, native CAP severity, normalized Meteoalarm awareness type, and CAP identifier under alerts/intl/meteoalarm/meteoalarm/... The awareness_type axis is derived from the Meteoalarm awareness_type parameter label and normalized for MQTT topic safety.

Meteoalarm.WeatherWarning.name: WeatherWarning
Meteoalarm.WeatherWarning.description: A severe weather warning from the EUMETNET Meteoalarm system. Contains CAP-structured alert data with awareness level and type, geographic area with EMMA/NUTS geocodes, and multilingual info blocks.

schema.name: WeatherWarning
schema.description: A severe weather warning from the EUMETNET Meteoalarm system. Contains CAP-structured alert data with awareness level and type, geographic area with EMMA/NUTS geocodes, and multilingual info blocks.

identifier.description: The unique CAP alert identifier assigned by the issuing national meteorological service.

sender.description: The identifier of the issuing national meteorological service (e.g., 'opendata@dwd.de').

sent.description: The date and time when the warning was issued, in ISO-8601 format.

status.description: The CAP alert status. 'Actual' for real warnings, 'Test' for test messages.

msg_type.description: The CAP message type indicating the nature of the alert.

scope.description: The CAP scope of the alert. Typically 'Public' for weather warnings.

country.description: Country feed slug where the warning applies (for example germany or france). Matches the {country} MQTT topic axis.

event.description: The weather event description, typically in the national language of the issuing service (e.g., 'STURMBÖEN', 'Thunderstorm').

category.description: The CAP alert category. 'Met' for meteorological warnings.

severity.description: Native CAP severity level (Minor, Moderate, Severe, Extreme, or Unknown). Matches the {severity} MQTT topic axis without further bucketing.

urgency.description: The CAP urgency level indicating the time-frame for protective action.

certainty.description: The CAP certainty level indicating the confidence in the forecast.

headline.description: A brief human-readable headline summarizing the warning, often in the national language.

description.description: A detailed description of the weather warning, often in the national language.

instruction.description: Recommended protective actions for the public.

effective.description: The date and time when the warning becomes effective, in ISO-8601 format.

onset.description: The expected date and time of onset of the weather event, in ISO-8601 format.

expires.description: The date and time when the warning expires, in ISO-8601 format.

web.description: A URL to the full warning details on the issuing service's website.

contact.description: Contact information for the issuing meteorological service.

awareness_level.description: The Meteoalarm awareness level as a string combining the numeric level and color, e.g., '2; yellow; Moderate'. Values range from '1; green' (no significant weather) to '4; red' (very dangerous).

awareness_type.description: Meteoalarm awareness type label normalized to lowercase kebab-case for MQTT topic routing (for example wind, snow-ice, thunderstorm, flooding). Matches the {awareness_type} MQTT topic axis.

area_desc.description: A textual description of the affected geographic area.

geocodes.description: A semicolon-separated list of geocode values (EMMA_ID or WARNCELLID) identifying the specific warning zones.

language.description: The language of the info block used to populate this event (e.g., 'de-DE', 'en-GB').

awareness_type_raw.description: Raw Meteoalarm awareness_type parameter value as provided by CAP, for example "2; Snow/Ice".
